mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, intensifying risk aversion and flight-to-safety demand for U.S. Treasuries, which drives long-term yields lower over a 20-trading-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment rose from 4.9% in Jan 2008 to 6.1% in Sept 2008
failure_condition: If UNRATE increases by 0.5% or more over a month and the 20-day forward Treasury yield does not decrease (i.e., yield is flat or positive), the hypothesis is rejected. Also, if correlation between UNRATE changes and yield changes is not negative over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls to or below 2%, it signals that the Fed has entered emergency easing mode, compressing short-term rates and pulling down long-term yields via the expectations hypothesis and portfolio rebalancing, leading to lower 10-year yields over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed cut rate to 1.5% on Oct 8, 2008
failure_condition: If on occasions when FEDFUNDS <= 2.0%, the 5-day forward Treasury yield rises more than 10% of the time (in walk-forward test), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) provide a dual signal of collapsing aggregate demand and labor income, which intensifies expectations of prolonged recession and further monetary easing, thus driving 10-year Treasury yields lower over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and PAYEMS show month-over-month decline
failure_condition: If in periods where both series decline concurrently, the subsequent 60-day Treasury yield decrease is not statistically significant (t-test p>0.10) compared to periods without concurrent decline, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: During a deep recession regime characterized by the federal funds rate at or below the unemployment rate (Taylor rule violation) and unemployment above 6%, the economy is in a liquidity trap-like environment where further easing expectations dominate, causing 10-year yields to drift lower over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: deep recession with low policy rates and high unemployment
failure_condition: If the regime condition holds but the 20-day forward yield increases in more than 40% of observations, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 900 thousand units, it indicates a severe housing market collapse that deepens the recession and depresses long-term growth and inflation expectations, leading to lower 10-year yields over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If after triggers, the 60-day forward yield decreases less than 20 basis points on average or shows positive returns in more than half the cases, the hypothesis is invalid.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing activity and falling capacity utilization, which signals lower economic growth ahead, reducing real yields and causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO declined from 111.18 in Aug 2008 to 110.30 in Sept 2008
failure_condition: If the correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and 20-day yield changes is not consistently negative (rolling 2-year rank IC <0) the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index declines month-over-month, it triggers disinflation fears and expectations of further Fed easing, which lowers real interest rate expectations and drives 10-year yields down over a 5-day window.
evidence_refs: fact:Sept 2008 CPI was 218.88 down from 219.086 in Aug 2008
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward yield does not decline by at least 5 bps on average following negative CPI prints over the test period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and housing starts both contract month-over-month, it signals a synchronized demand collapse that strongly depresses growth expectations, leading to a steep decline in 10-year yields over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST show month-over-month decline
failure_condition: If the average yield decline in 60-day windows following concurrent declines is less than 30 bps or not statistically different from non-trigger periods (p>0.05), reject hypothesis.
